portal-glimpse.config.title: Portal Glimpse
portal-glimpse.config.glimpsesVisible.to: Master toggle for the mod. Turn off for plain, 100% vanilla nether portals.
portal-glimpse.config.entityOverPanorama: A player standing in a portal is drawn through the glimpse, so they look like they're standing in the destination dimension.
portal-glimpse.config.shaderRenderMethod: How the glimpse draws while an Iris shaderpack is active (no effect without shaders). RTT renders it through the shader pipeline so it's lit/PBR'd like the world; Overlay draws it on top after the shaders (reliable, but not shader-lit).
portal-glimpse.shaderpack.unsupported: Portal Glimpse: the shaderpack "%s" isn't supported yet — the RTT glimpse has no calibration for it and may look too dark, washed out or blown out. BSL is the recommended pack for now.
portal-glimpse.unsupported.title: This shaderpack isn't supported yet
portal-glimpse.unsupported.line1: The shader-lit glimpse (RTT) has no calibration for this pack,
portal-glimpse.unsupported.line2: so it may look too dark, washed out or blown out.
portal-glimpse.unsupported.line3: You can suggest this pack to the author to get it supported.
portal-glimpse.unsupported.useOverlay: Use Overlay instead (works on any pack)
portal-glimpse.config.shaderpackStatus.u: Shaderpack: %s — NOT SUPPORTED YET (uncalibrated; may look too dark or blown out)
portal-glimpse.config.godRayOccluder.too: RTT only. Hides an invisible cage of solid blocks behind the glimpse so a shaderpack's sun shafts (god rays) stop there instead of shining through the portal. Turn off to see whether the rays pierce the glimpse on their own. Never replaces real blocks.
portal-glimpse.config.rttPrediction.tool: RTT only: how strongly the panorama predicts your camera motion to cancel the 1-frame lag on fast head movement. 100% = full prediction (smoothest when moving steadily); lower it if quick flicks overshoot or wobble.
portal-glimpse.config.panoramaFov.toolti: How much of the destination the portal frames. Higher = a wider view with smaller-looking content.
Swirl opacity over the Nether view — what you see looking into portals while in the Overworld. 0% = clear window, 100% = full vanilla swirl.
portal-glimpse.config.overworldVeilOpaci: Swirl opacity over the Overworld view — what you see looking into portals while in the Nether. 0% = clear window, 100% = full vanilla swirl.
portal-glimpse.config.captureCooldown.to: Minimum time before a portal re-captures its auto glimpse on travel. Off = capture every eligible trip.
portal-glimpse.config.captureRadius.tool: Chunks in each direction that must load around the arrival portal before an auto capture. Higher = more terrain in the glimpse, but the loading screen is held longer. 0 = capture as soon as vanilla terrain is ready.
portal-glimpse.preview.dragHint: Drag left / right to look around
